parsed_review.summary: CP00-807 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk B, 40 units RP26.P06.M03.S09-RP26.P06.M04.S18) is a descriptor-only pack. Verified by direct source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only; no Bash/commands executed): (1) createEnterpriseSaasCp807PermissionMatrixWorkflowTailDescriptor (packages/enterprise/src/service.js:1406) exists, sources from the CP806 descriptor, and carries the correct authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). (2) Coverage matches the live plan exactly: deliverable counts implementation:15/ui:8/claude_review:3/security_audit:9/test:4/hermes_evidence:1 = 40, micro-title split 22/18; row math reconciles (M03 = PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS.slice(8,20)=12 + 2 test rows + 8 evidence/handoff rows = 22; M04 = slice(0,18)=18). validateCoverage (validators.js:122) and validateDescriptor (validators.js:158) perform real, non-vacuous assertions, and model.test.js:820-883 runs them against the actual docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (planPack helper reads the real file). (3) No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries are enforced: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:392) and every CP807 ROW_EXTRAS entry (service.js:66-257) set runtime flags false; validateDescriptor rejects any row asserting runtime permission evaluation, permission/audit writes, UI/API runtime, bypass execution, cross-tenant routes, secret/key material, or enterprise trust. (4) Hermes is used only for a descriptor-only evidence packet (createEnterpriseSaasCp807HermesEvidencePacket: writes_product_state=false, emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write); Claude packet is read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2=true. (5) Contract/projection/plan/README are mutually consistent (contract latest_pack=CP00-807, latest_projection + projections.cp807 descriptor match, validation.valid=true; plan pack CP00-807 deviation_from_plan=false; README CP00-807 section accurate; index.js re-exports all CP807 symbols used by the validate script). The manifest makes no premature claim: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, status=in_progress, pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending, valid_review_receipts=[] - fully consistent with the prompt authority boundary (enterprise trust closed, human approval required, Hermes not embedded in product runtime, Claude read-only and non-final). Unrelated dirty files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, workbook/*, the .docx, LDIP result) are correctly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and excluded from active pack scope, so they are not treated as part of CP807. Review readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON (non-zero stdout); no malformed/0-byte/auth-failure or prior invalid review evidence exists. As read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), I find no P0/P1/P2 issues; the bounded descriptor-verified completion claim is supported. One P3 documents that downstream closeout artifacts and the validation-command run are not yet present/executed - expected at this review stage and outside what a read-only review can confirm.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts and validation-command evidence not yet present (expected at the Claude-review stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-807/. pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending with valid_review_receipts=[]. This is the expected state during the Claude review step: these artifacts, plus execution of the listed validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check, etc.), are produced/run by the closeout pipeline after this review. Being read-only, I verified the implementation, registry binding, validators, tests, contract projection, plan entry, README, and no-write/runtime-closed boundaries by source inspection only; I did not and cannot execute commands or confirm downstream adjudication/inspection. No defect in the pack's current claims was found - the manifest correctly reports production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / in_progress and does not overclaim.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the normal closeout pipeline: run the listed validation commands, capture command-evidence.json, and generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json; then obtain human final approval before any runtime or enterprise-trust opening (deferred to CP00-808 / RP26.P06.M04.S19). No code change required for this pack.
